metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Convert AC2 to an in-session deliverable: specify that a measurement-framework artifact (a tracking comment template, a scheduled follow-up ticket, or a dashboard query) is created at merge time, and record actual field data as a separate, explicitly deferred follow-up item with a stated time bound.
findings.finding: AC2 ('A follow-up measurement note records post-change field data: code-review block/false-block counts, and plan-review advisory-uptake behavior after the guidance loosening') is explicitly post-sprint — it requires observing multi-day field behavior after deployment and cannot be evaluated in-session.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Split AC2 into: (a) an in-session AC — 'A follow-up tracking ticket is opened with the measurement template (metrics: block count, false-block count, advisory uptake %) and a stated review date of T+30 days'; (b) a deferred note — 'Field data will be recorded in that ticket; it is not a gate on this epic's closure.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Assign AC2 (post-change measurement note on the epic) to a specific child ticket as an explicit acceptance criterion, or add a ninth child that owns the measurement observation and comment.
findings.finding: AC2 of the parent ('a follow-up measurement note on the epic records post-change field data: code-review block/false-block counts and advisory-uptake behavior') is not owned as an acceptance criterion by any child. e1f2 mentions recording an observation plan in its What section, but its ACs do not commit to posting the measurement comment on the epic. b9c0's replay AC is pre-merge, not post-go-live field data. No child AC requires the durable post-change field measurement note that completes the parent's AC2.
findings.location: parent AC2; no single child
findings.suggested_fix: Add to e1f2's ACs: '- [ ] A comment is posted on the parent epic with post-change advisory-uptake data (norm_id survival rate before/after) measured from REVIEW_RESULT sidecars after the guidance change is live.' Add to b9c0's ACs: '- [ ] A comment is posted on the parent epic with post-go-live code-review block/false-block counts measured from the first N changes after the threshold flip.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit AC (or expand AC-b) that verifies SC3's deliverables: (a) completion-verifier emits a single durable FAIL enumerating all unmet criteria, and (b) the signed-children anti-pattern is documented in an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry.
findings.finding: Success Criterion 3 describes two concrete deliverables — (a) durably-persisted FAIL verdicts that enumerate all unmet criteria in one verdict, and (b) documentation of the 'signed-children AC anti-pattern' — but neither AC-a (umbrella child-tickets) nor AC-b (measurement note) explicitly verifies these outcomes. AC-b's measurement scope is limited to 'code-review block/false-block counts' and 'plan-review advisory-uptake behavior', omitting completion-verifier correctness entirely.
findings.location: Success Criteria §3 vs Acceptance Criteria AC-b
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] Completion-verifier fix is validated: CI/log evidence shows a single FAIL verdict enumerating all unmet criteria (no piecemeal ratchet); the signed-children AC anti-pattern is recorded in ADR-XXXX or CLAUDE.md.' to the Acceptance Criteria block.

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the 'serious-tier criteria' enumeration (or replace with explicit criterion IDs / a criterion attribute) so the blocking-lane enablement child has an unambiguous scope.
findings.finding: The Success Criterion 1 phrase 'enabled for serious-tier criteria' references an undefined tier-grouping enum. No definition of 'serious-tier' exists anywhere in the codebase: not in the code-review criteria_routing.json, not in ADR-0036, not in the review-kernel, not in any ADR. The code-review routing uses blocking_enabled:true only on specific named DET-security criterion IDs; no 'tier' taxonomy is defined or referenced elsewhere.
findings.location: Success Criteria, criterion 1: 'enabled for serious-tier criteria'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'serious-tier criteria' with an explicit list of criterion IDs (e.g., the existing DET-security entries plus any LLM criteria above the derived threshold) or define a 'tier: serious' attribute in criteria_routing.json that the derivation child (9f25) populates.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] b9c0 should defer the criteria list (not just the threshold) to 9f25's output, or 9f25 should explicitly adjudicate per-criterion precision in addition to per-threshold precision, producing a formal approved-criteria list that b9c0 consumes.
findings.finding: Child b9c0 pre-commits a specific named set of serious-tier criteria to enable (security, data_loss_without_recovery, security_bypass_not_enforced_elsewhere, silent_wrong_feeding_a_decision, unversioned_published_contract_break, safety_net_removal_without_replacement) in its What section, independently of what sibling 9f25's empirical precision analysis may conclude about each criterion's individual block-worthiness. If 9f25's adjudication shows that one or more named criteria have precision < 0.5 at any candidate threshold, b9c0 has no mechanism to remove them from the approved blocking set — producing a blocking configuration that contradicts the evidence it depends on.
findings.location: child b9c0-2b03-36de-469f What section; child 9f25-ca33-74de-40bf
findings.suggested_fix: Extend 9f25's scope to produce a per-criterion precision table alongside the threshold crossover, and update b9c0's What to say 'enable the criteria approved by the 9f25 adjudication table' rather than naming them upfront.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm that flattering-ethologic-cassowary (leaf-plan parent-containment criterion) is traceable to a calibration session finding, or move it to a separate epic so the calibration epic's scope matches its three stated Success Criteria.
findings.finding: The plan-review criterion addition in flattering-ethologic-cassowary (leaf-plan parent-containment) is a new plan-review rule unrelated to the three calibration goals stated in the epic's Success Criteria and Context, which focus on code-review blocking, advisory guidance, and completion-verifier fixes. It introduces a second 'reason to change' (plan-review criterion authoring) with no stated rationale tying it to the calibration session findings.
findings.location: DECOMPOSITION STATE — child flattering-ethologic-cassowary

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Co-locate the crossover derivation rule with ADR-0036 (or a new ADR/appendix) and explicitly state how it relates to the existing HIGH↔NIT A/B gate — are both required, is one a proxy for the other, or does one supersede the other?
findings.finding: The code-review blocking threshold derivation rule stated in the plan — 'crossover threshold where a finding is more likely correctly-blocking than false-blocking' / 'smallest t where P(correctly blocking)>0.5, prior 0.6' — is not co-located with the A/B gate schema (ADR-0036 / ab_impact_model.py) that must implement it. ADR-0036's A/B gate tests HIGH↔NIT median separation (a model-discrimination criterion), not a P(correctly-blocking) crossover. These are logically distinct decision criteria and neither references the other.
findings.location: Success Criteria, criterion 1; also session log shaggy-linoleum-gavial comment 2

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm d4cf's membership in this epic is intentional, OR move it to a separate epic scoped to plan-review criteria expansion; if retained, add a parent SC/AC that explicitly commissions the G7 leaf-plan criterion.
findings.finding: Child d4cf (G7 leaf-plan parent-containment criterion) introduces scope that is not commissioned by any parent success or acceptance criterion. The parent's context explicitly enumerates three problems from the 2026-07-12 calibration analysis — code-review too loose, advisory-remediation mandate, and completion-verifier defects — and the parent's success criteria and ACs are bounded to those three. A new plan-review criterion for leaf-plan scope drift is a fourth concern not mentioned anywhere in the parent. No other sibling covers this scope either, meaning it is an unclaimed addition rather than a gap-fill.
findings.location: child d4cf-0780-cbed-4879
findings.suggested_fix: If d4cf is intentional, add a fourth success criterion to the parent: 'A leaf-plan parent-containment criterion (G7) is added to the plan-review gate to catch leaf-plan scope drift at claim time.' Otherwise, detach d4cf to its own epic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback / back-out procedure for the code-review blocking lane (e.g., 'revert threshold to advisory-only if false-block rate exceeds X% in the first N post-flip rounds') so the reversibility mechanism is documented, not just the gate condition.
findings.finding: The code-review blocking-lane flip has no explicit rollback path. The plan gates the flip on the ADR-0036 A/B test but says nothing about what happens if post-flip monitoring shows regression (e.g., false-block surge). Acknowledgement alone ('passes the A/B gate before flipping') is insufficient per T4 — the remedy must include a reversibility mechanism.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'Code-review blocking … passes the ADR-0036 A/B gate before flipping'
findings.suggested_fix: In the Success Criteria or a child ticket, state: 'If post-flip false-block rate exceeds [threshold] within [observation window], the blocking lane reverts to advisory-only via [config toggle / feature flag / config-file change] and a new A/B cycle is required before re-enabling.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the fix beyond documentation: either update the epic template / CLAUDE.md guidance to omit '(signed)' from the child-closure AC, or add a plan-review check that flags 'all children closed (signed)' as a dod_uncertifiable pattern when operator-attested children are present.
findings.finding: The plan's fix for the 'signed-children AC anti-pattern' is documentation only, which does not prevent recurrence. The plan says the anti-pattern is 'documented,' but: (a) the plan's own AC1 ('All child tickets are closed (the epic's work items)') reproduces a softer form of the same pattern — it omits '(signed)' but the completion-verifier will still evaluate child closure via the certifiable:false path; (b) agents following the epic template in CLAUDE.md and CONTRIBUTING.md will continue authoring epics with 'all child stories closed (signed)' ACs unless the guidance or gate is changed. Documentation in a bug ticket is not surfaced at epic-authoring time.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'the signed-children AC anti-pattern is documented'; also Context — 'children closed (signed) epic ACs defeat the certifiable:false escape hatch (bf50/5f39 contagion, bug 02a3)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit ordering constraint: 9f25's corpus analysis and b9c0's enablement must use a pure code-v2 cohort (pre-f32e); the code-v3 cohort is a separate future calibration exercise. State this in both 9f25 and f32e's ACs or scope sections.
findings.finding: Children f32e (code-v3 IMPACT_MODEL_VERSION bump) and 9f25+b9c0 (threshold derivation and enablement using the code-v2 corpus) create an ordering hazard: once f32e ships and new sidecars carry code-v3 scores, the threshold derived by 9f25 from the code-v2 corpus may not be valid for the code-v3 score distribution. b9c0's replay AC requires showing '#518-class and #539-class findings now BLOCK' but does not specify whether the replay uses code-v2 or code-v3 scores. Neither f32e nor 9f25 states an explicit ordering or version-cohort boundary.
findings.location: child f32e-53cf-7db5-4781; child 9f25-ca33-74de-40bf; child b9c0-2b03-36de-469f

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace AC1's signed-children pattern with a concrete, named list of child tickets (or a query/label that enumerates them), consistent with the anti-pattern documentation goal in SC3.
findings.finding: AC1 ('All child tickets are closed') uses the exact 'signed-children AC anti-pattern' the plan itself identifies as a structural bug (bug 02a3, bf50/5f39 contagion) — creating a direct internal contradiction where the plan both documents the anti-pattern and instantiates it.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1 / Context paragraph (bug 02a3 reference)
findings.suggested_fix: Replace AC1 with: '- [ ] Child tickets [T-NNN, T-NNN, T-NNN] (code-review threshold ticket, guidance-loosening ticket, verifier-fix ticket) are each individually closed with artifacts cited — do NOT use a generic signed-children gate (anti-pattern documented in SC3).' Name the actual child ticket IDs.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the child ticket IDs (or a deterministic label/query) in AC1 so an executor can enumerate and verify closure without external clarification.
findings.finding: AC1 does not name or reference the child tickets anywhere in the plan, so an executor cannot enumerate 'the epic's work items' without external lookup — the scope boundary is undefined.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC1

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that explicitly verifies the ADR-0036 A/B gate outcome before the code-review blocking flip is considered complete (e.g., 'A/B gate result is recorded and shows blocking is more likely correctly-blocking than false-blocking for serious-tier criteria').
findings.finding: Success Criterion 1 conditions the code-review blocking flip on passing 'the ADR-0036 A/B gate', but no Acceptance Criterion verifies that this gate was actually passed before blocking was enabled. AC-a ('all child tickets closed') is an output gate, not a process gate, so the A/B gate condition in SC1 is unmeasured at epic closure.
findings.location: Success Criteria §1 vs Acceptance Criteria
findings.suggested_fix: Add: '- [ ] ADR-0036 A/B gate result is documented (link to gate artefact); blocking is only enabled if the gate confirms the crossover threshold is met.' to the Acceptance Criteria block.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a child ticket or AC line explicitly creating/updating ADR-0036 to capture the blocking-threshold methodology, the serious-tier criteria selection rationale, and the A/B gate design, so the decision is navigable and not buried in session logs.
findings.finding: ADR-0036 is referenced as the governing architectural record for the blocking-lane change, but the plan does not confirm whether ADR-0036 will be created or updated as part of this work. The derivation methodology (crossover threshold from adjudicated field data, serious-tier criteria selection) constitutes a new architectural decision that requires a traceable ADR entry.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'passes the ADR-0036 A/B gate before flipping'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: '- [ ] ADR-0036 is created or updated to document the crossover-threshold derivation, the serious-tier criteria set, and the A/B gate success condition.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the derived blocking threshold is stored in a configurable parameter (e.g., a config file or feature-flag store) so it can be updated from field data without a code change.
findings.finding: The code-review blocking threshold ('crossover threshold where a finding is more likely correctly-blocking than false-blocking') is described as derived from field data and expected to be re-calibrated over time, but the plan does not specify that this threshold value will be externalized/configurable rather than hardcoded — risking a code change every time the threshold needs updating.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'Code-review blocking is derived from adjudicated field data (the crossover threshold…)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a line to the Success Criteria or child-ticket scope: 'The crossover threshold value is read from [config location] so re-calibration from new field data requires only a config/parameter update, not a code deployment.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add one AC per Success Criterion that directly verifies its end-state: (SC1) confirm ADR-0036 A/B gate passed and threshold documented; (SC2) confirm CLAUDE.md diff shows advisory language changed from 'MUST remediate' to judgment language; (SC3) confirm verifier integration test demonstrates single-verdict FAIL with all unmet criteria enumerated.
findings.finding: The Acceptance Criteria do not directly verify the three Success Criteria end-states: AC1 ('all child tickets closed') is a closure proxy that passes even if the code-review threshold is miscalibrated, the advisory-guidance CLAUDE.md change was never made, or the completion-verifier still emits piecemeal FAILs. Each non-deferred goal needs at least one end-state-observable proof at the AC level.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria (both ACs)
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace or augment AC1 with three targeted ACs:
- [ ] ADR-0036 A/B gate result is recorded and blocking threshold is documented in the code-review config.
- [ ] CLAUDE.md (or equivalent guidance file) diff shows advisory remediation changed from mandatory to judgment-based.
- [ ] Completion-verifier emits a single FAIL verdict listing all unmet criteria (demonstrated by an integration test or run log).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit 'blocked-by' or sequencing note (in the epic or in compliant-unamorous-tiger) stating that the lane-enablement ticket must not start until acidic-undead-finwhale has delivered the adjudicated threshold.
findings.finding: The sequencing dependency between acidic-undead-finwhale (derive block threshold) and compliant-unamorous-tiger (enable blocking lane at that threshold) is not made explicit anywhere in the epic or in either child, creating a risk that the lane is enabled before the threshold is empirically derived.
findings.location: DECOMPOSITION STATE — children acidic-undead-finwhale and compliant-unamorous-tiger; Success Criteria bullet 1
findings.suggested_fix: Add one line to compliant-unamorous-tiger's description: 'Depends on acidic-undead-finwhale; use the derived threshold value as input — do not begin until that ticket is closed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the concrete storage mechanism for durable FAIL verdicts (e.g. a new `completion-verifier-fail` attestation kind, a sidecar analogous to REVIEW_RESULT, or a store event) so child implementers have a grounded, consistent design target.
findings.finding: The plan commits to 'durably persisted' FAIL verdicts for the completion-verifier but specifies no storage mechanism. Currently, a FAIL verdict raises CommandError and is never written to the ticket store (no event, no sidecar, no attestation kind). The plan describes the desired invariant but leaves the data-flow entirely open: is persistence a new event type, a failed-close sidecar analogous to REVIEW_RESULT, an attestation with verdict=FAIL, or a comment? Without this, child implementers have no grounded design to execute.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'Completion-verifier FAILs are durably persisted and enumerate all unmet criteria in one verdict'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale explaining why the impact-score crossover threshold is the right approach for code-review blocking (e.g. because it accounts for trigger likelihood and reversibility multipliers that bare consequence-binary blocking ignores).
findings.finding: The plan's positive rationale for the code-review blocking approach is absent. The Success Criteria describe the crossover-threshold mechanism (adjudicated field data, serious-tier criteria, ADR-0036 A/B gate) but never explain why this is preferred over structurally simpler alternatives — most notably, blocking directly on the consequence-binary attributes that already encode severity intent (e.g. `data_loss_without_recovery=True` → block, bypassing the impact score entirely). Without a stated rationale, future calibration decisions lack a documented basis for whether the threshold-based path or a binary-based override is appropriate.
findings.location: Success Criteria — 'Code-review blocking is derived from adjudicated field data (the crossover threshold where a finding is more likely correctly-blocking than false-blocking), enabled for serious-tier criteria, and passes the ADR-0036 A/B gate before flipping.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Success Criteria or Context: 'The threshold-based approach is preferred over direct consequence-binary blocking because the impact model's trigger-likelihood and reversibility multipliers correctly dampen rare/reversible serious findings that would be false-blocks at the binary level.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a fallback protocol for the A/B gate failure case (e.g., 'if gate fails after N weeks, document the corpus-size gap and defer to next calibration cycle') so child ticket 9f25 has a defined exit in all outcomes.
findings.finding: No fallback protocol is specified for the case where the ADR-0036 A/B gate fails to justify a threshold-down flip. The plan states the blocking-lane flip 'passes the ADR-0036 A/B gate before flipping,' but does not say what happens if the gate exits non-zero: whether the child ticket stays open indefinitely, whether an alternative threshold is tried, or whether the blocking lane stays permanently off.
findings.location: Success Criteria, criterion 1; child tickets 9f25 → b9c0 in session log shaggy-linoleum-gavial

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a cross-reference between 24ec and 74d9 specifying that the findings[] schema (fields, multiplicity) is agreed before implementation; 24ec's persistence layer must handle N>=1 findings with at least the 'criterion' field that 74d9's prompt mandates.
findings.finding: Children 24ec and 74d9 share the completion-verifier verdict data structure (the findings[] payload) but neither lists the other as a dependency, and neither explicitly coordinates on the schema. 74d9 mandates exhaustive per-criterion findings (one finding per unmet criterion), potentially expanding the findings[] array and the fields each entry must carry; 24ec persists the verdict as-received. If 74d9 changes the findings schema (e.g., adds a 'criterion' field per entry) after 24ec has already established its persistence format, the persisted records from 24ec may be structurally incomplete.
findings.location: child 24ec-c291-9f47-47fd and child 74d9-f4e2-2d4a-4cc6
findings.suggested_fix: In 24ec, explicitly state that findings[] items follow the schema produced by the exhaustive-evaluation prompt (74d9), and add a test asserting a two-finding FAIL verdict is persisted with both findings intact. Mark 74d9 as a soft dependency or shared-schema coordinate.

findings.finding: The plan silently drops child ticket f32e (impact_code lane gap: untested-degradation / unmasked-regression findings score impact 0.0). The session log lists this as a discrete child work item, the plan's Context cites impact 0.0 only as evidence of code-review looseness, and no Success Criterion or Acceptance Criterion commits to fixing the scoring gap itself.

The plan silently drops child ticket d4cf (Plan-review criterion: leaf-plan parent-containment — leaf scope must be a subset of parent). The session log lists this as a discrete child work item; the plan's narrative, Success Criteria, and Acceptance Criteria contain no mention of adding this criterion.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
